Essential Baking Guide: Successful baking depends on accurate measurement, proper ingredient temperature, and understanding how leavening agents work. Butter and eggs at room temperature incorporate more evenly into batters, producing a better texture. Baking soda (sodium bicarbonate) requires an acidic ingredient to activate, while baking powder contains its own acid and is double-acting, releasing gas twice — once when wet and again when heated. Overmixing develops gluten in all-purpose flour, leading to tough cakes and muffins; mix only until ingredients are just combined when a tender crumb is desired.
